hypothesis: For each listed retained true-prefix length L in {1,2,4,8,16,32,64,128,256}, replacing every center bit c_t with zero for t >= L produces a nonzero reconstructed initial-left bit by depth 500; the exported period-length 1 through 3, width-4 transition graphs exactly enumerate their stated finite models.
interpretation: All nine fully specified prefix-then-zero traces have exact finite incompatibility witnesses. The graph artifacts exactly enumerate 14 fixed-width, zero-outer-boundary functional graphs. Neither finite calculation proves eventual center-column nonperiodicity or supplies a depth-independent finite-state reduction.
limitations: Only the nine explicitly listed retained-prefix lengths were tested.
A finite incompatibility witness concerns one fully specified trace only.
The width-4 graphs force the outer right boundary to zero.
The graph state bound grows as period_length times 2**width.
No finite search proves eventual nonperiodicity.
proof_scope: Exact finite Rule 30 inversion for nine specified center traces through depth 500 and exact enumeration of 14 width-4 functional graphs for all binary word descriptions of lengths 1 through 3.
question: problem1

software.algorithm: rule30lab.sideways packed temporal-column reconstruction plus deterministic fixed-width graph export
